system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;

        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{

    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Persistent Errors Across All Attempts

Both errors persist from Attempt 1 through Attempt 7:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Various forms of the "face does not have neighbour cell" error for the outlet face at x=2.
2. **`log.pimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — purely consequential.

## Root Cause: Fundamental Geometry/Mesh Topology Issue

After carefully analyzing all attempts, the core problem is that **Blocks 1 and 2 (the lower and upper blocks flanking the cylinder arm) are NOT geometrically connected** — they occupy disjoint y-ranges ([-0.5, -0.15] and [0.15, 0.5]) with a 0.3-unit gap between them. No block spans the gap region y ∈ [-0.15, 0.15].

Every attempt to fix the outlet face or fix block windings has failed because the underlying mesh topology is fundamentally flawed — the blocks don't share internal faces, so the mesh cannot form a valid connected volume.

## Proposed Solution: Restructure the Mesh with Proper Connectivity

The standard offsetCylinder tutorial uses **three blocks** where the middle block bridges the gap. Here’s the correct approach:

### 1. `system/blockMeshDict` — Redefine blocks with proper connectivity

Keep Block 0 (upstream) and Block 3 (downstream) as-is. Replace Blocks 1 and 2 with a **single middle block** that spans the gap region and connects everything:

- **Block 0** (unchanged): `hex (0 4 7 3 1 2 5 6)` — x∈[-1,-0.2], y∈[-0.5,0.5], z∈[0,-0.1]
- **Middle Block** (new): `hex (11 14 17 16 15 10 21 20)` — x∈[-0.2,0.2], y∈[-0.15, 0.15], z∈[0,-0.1]  
- **Block 3** (unchanged): `hex (28 24 25 29 31 26 27 30)` — x∈[0.2,2], y∈[-0.5,0.5], z∈[0,-0.1]

The middle block `hex (11 14 17 16 15 10 21 20)` fills the gap from y=-0.15 to y=0.15, connecting Block 0 (which goes to y=0.5) with Block 3 (which goes to y=-0.5). Its six faces become:
- Bottom (y=-0.15): (11, 14, 17, 16) — external, becomes part of `cylinder` patch
- Top (y=0.15): (15, 10, 21, 20) — external, becomes part of `cylinder` patch  
- Left (x=-0.2): (16, 11, 15, 20) — internal, shared with nothing (exposed as wall or kept internal)
- Right (x=0.2): (17, 14, 10, 21) — internal, shared with nothing
- Front (z=0): (11, 16, 20, 15) — external, becomes `bottom` or `top` depending on naming
- Back (z=-0.1): (14, 17, 21, 10) — external, becomes `bottom` or `top`

Set the `cylinder` patch to include the four vertical faces: `(11 14 17 16)`, `(15 10 21 20)`, plus the corresponding z=-0.1 faces `(14 11 20 15)` and `(17 14 10 21)`.

Remove the old Blocks 1 and 2 and the standalone `outlet` boundary. Add the far-right face `(30 31 25 24)` to the `cylinder` patch as a `wall`.

### 2. `0/U` and `0/p` — Update boundary patches

Ensure the boundary condition files reference the correct patch names that will emerge from the new blockMesh topology (likely `inlet`, `cylinder`, `top`, `bottom`, `farOutlet` or similar, matching what blockMeshDict defines).

## Summary

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Replace the two disconnected blocks (1 and 2) with a single middle bridge block that spans y∈[-0.15, 0.15] and properly connects the upstream/downstream regions. Set the `cylinder` patch to include all four vertical faces of this new block. Remove the erroneous `outlet` entry.
2. **`0/U` and `0/p`**: Verify patch names match the new blockMesh output and update accordingly.</review_analysis>

Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 0.01 in physical properties.</user_requirement>
